programme.title: Deadliest Catch
programme.desc: A massive arctic hurricane shocks the fleet with high winds and big waves. An unstable stack threatens to capsize the Saga, as the captains rush to pull and set pots before harbour.

programme.title: Deadliest Catch
programme.desc: Sig seeks retribution for his son-in-law's prank. Jake faces unforeseen challenges in freakish weather. Josh and Casey make a bet. Wild Bill fights to make his last delivery.

programme.title: Curse of the Bermuda Triangle
programme.desc: To find the truth behind unexplained phenomena, experts follow new clues to underwater alien anomalies that some believe have ties to the lost city of Atlantis.

programme.title: Expedition Unknown
programme.desc: Josh scours Virginia backcountry in search of the legendary Thomas Beale Treasure, worth millions today. With encrypted codes hiding its whereabouts, Josh takes a detour to Philadelphia to uncover missing pieces to the puzzle.

programme.title: Expedition Unknown
programme.desc: Josh travels to UK and Norway to unlock the secrets of the Viking Sunstone, a mystic crystal the Vikings used to conquer the seas. Before testing it, he explores a crypt in a Nordic church and descends 1000ft into a deserted mine.

programme.title: Homestead Rescue
programme.desc: A hungry bear scratching the walls, disease carrying ticks, no food to eat, no power and 60 massive oaks looming over a flimsy cabin in tornado alley present the Raneys with their greatest challenges yet.

programme.title: Homestead Rescue
programme.desc: Amputee Jeff and wife Carol need a home, water, and a place to grow food. The Raneys build an entire cabin from scratch and harness a natural spring.

programme.title: Opal Hunters: Red Dirt Road Trip
programme.desc: Rod and JC prospect a long-defunct diamond mine. Plus, saving an orphaned baby wallaby leads to important volunteer work for Rod and JC. And, in Broome, their rough diamonds are transformed into $10,000 worth of cut stones.

programme.title: Opal Hunters: Red Dirt Road Trip
programme.desc: Rod and JC team up with Kakadu National Park Rangers to catalogue the world's biggest species of crocodile. Plus, Rod finds a monster ruby at the isolated, gem-filled Harts Range.

programme.title: Deadliest Catch
programme.desc: Fishing crews on the Bering Sea endure mortal peril and intense discomfort during crab season, with 12-metre waves, 318-kilogram crab pots that can easily crush a careless crewman, and freezing temperatures around the clock.

programme.title: Deadliest Catch
programme.desc: It's the deadliest job in the world: crab fishing off the Alaska coast in the icy Bering Sea, home of the most violent waters on earth. In this opening episode, it's the first day of work as 1,500 fishermen converge at Dutch Harbor, Alaska.

programme.title: The Land Girls
programme.desc: Three very different girls work in the Land Army, the volunteer force that replaced agricultural workers called up for service in the Second World War. Sent to a Dorset farm, they all fall for the landowner's son and the scene is then set for romance, heartbreak and tragedy.

programme.title: Erin Brockovich
programme.desc: Based on the true story of an unsophisticated single mother who stumbles upon an environmental cover-up. While working for a lawyer, she is puzzled by an old case and decides to do some research of her own. She discovers that a chemical company knowingly polluted the local area, and with the help of the lawyer launches a class action to sue the company for damages.

programme.title: Bombshell
programme.desc: A revealing look inside the most powerful and controversial media empire of all time and the explosive story of the women who brought down the infamous man who created it.

programme.desc: Mickey O'Neill wants the famous Josef Locke to perform at his nightclub. O'Neill's reputation was ruined when he hired an impostor claiming to be Locke and only a performance by the real Locke can make up for it.

programme.title: Send Me No Flowers
programme.desc: George Kimball mistakenly believes he only has a few months to live, and arouses the suspicions of his wife Judy when he tries to pair her off with Bert Power as a potential future husband.

programme.title: The Last Wagon
programme.desc: Todd is an orphaned son of white settlers bought up by the Comanche. When his wife and children are killed by three brothers, he exacts appropriate revenge. Caught and charged with murder, he's sent by wagon train to face justice, but when the train is attacked by Apaches, he is the only male adult survivor and has to escort a rag-tag band of survivors to safety while the law set off in pursuit of their quarry.

programme.title: Decision at Sundown
programme.desc: A gunman is obsessed with tracking down and killing the man who stole his tramp wife. However, a gun duel reveals the truth.

programme.title: Nope
programme.desc: A man and his sister discover something sinister in the skies above their California horse ranch, while the owner of a nearby theme park tries to profit from the mysterious, otherworldly phenomenon.

programme.title: Monolith
programme.desc: While trying to salvage her career, a disgraced journalist begins investigating a strange conspiracy theory. However, as the trail leads uncomfortably close to home, she's left to grapple with the lies at the heart of her own story.

programme.title: Paul Hollywood City Bakes
programme.desc: Paul gets a guided tour around Cape Town's best baking hotspots from fellow Food Network star Siba Mtongana. Later, Paul creates two bakes of his own: Earl Grey milk tart and BBQ meat pasties.

programme.title: Mary Berry's Everyday
programme.desc: Mary travels to Scotland to enjoy the best of their everyday larder with recipes inspired by her own Scots heritage. She discovers wonderful shellfish in Ullapool, and shares the joy of venison cooked on a BBQ with chef Tom Kitchin.

programme.title: Mary Berry Everyday
programme.desc: Mary shares inspiring everyday dishes full of wholesome goodness from her secret tip for a rich bolognaise to a heart-warming soup. She dons a white suit and tries her hand at bee keeping, collecting fresh honey to make a winning pud.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Ree Drummond welcomes a new cowboy to the ranch with a frontier-style dinner kit of his favourite food, including short rib sandwiches, roasted butternut squash salad and salted caramel frosted brownies.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: It's comfort from start to finish and Ree's spelling it out in an old-fashioned, feel-good way. She cooks up tuna noodle casserole, meatballs in sweet and sour sauce, glazed apple dumplings and Welsh rarebit.

programme.title: Jamie's 15 Minute Meals
programme.sub-title: Rosemary Chicken and Happy Cow Burgers
programme.desc: On the menu are rosemary chicken, grilled polenta and porcini tomato sauce, plus burgers, old-school coleslaw and corn on the cob.

programme.title: Jamie's 15 Minute Meals
programme.sub-title: Sticky Squid Balls and Sausage Fusilli
programme.desc: Jamie cooks sticky squid balls, grilled prawns and noodle broth, plus sausage fusilli and creamy garden salad.

programme.title: Be My Guest With Ina Garten
programme.desc: Ina Garten welcomes stage and screen actor Laura Linney to her home with a fruity blueberry ricotta breakfast cake; in honour of Laura's Southern heritage, Ina teaches her how to make shrimp boil with a twist.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: The remaining bakers take on more challenges set by judges Paul Hollywood and Prue Leith in the nation's most famous tent, in a bid to be crowned star baker.

programme.title: Rachel Allen: Bake!
programme.desc: Rachel bakes a quiche lorraine, meets German `Konditormeister' Falko Burkert, and learns how to make a cake on a spit known as a baumkuchen, or tree-cake.

programme.title: James Martin's Great British Adventure
programme.desc: James continues his British adventure in Lancashire with top local chef Lisa Allen in the passenger seat of his trusty red mini. Their first stop is an incredible food hall in Lisa's home town of Clitheroe. Here they shop and then cook together a fantastic BBQ'd john dory with kohlrabi and fennel salad. Next, Lisa is in the driving seat as she takes the mini to a local buffalo farm where they're producing some interesting cheese from buffalo milk.

programme.title: James Martin's Great British Adventure
programme.desc: James' adventure brings him over the border into North Wales, where he's joined by chef Stephen Terry for this leg of his journey. Their first stop is the grand Pale Hall, where head chef Gareth Stevenson is cooking up a real treat - Moroccan-inspired Welsh lamb. James offers his own variation of Welsh lamb in the grounds of the hall, serving with gnocchi and vegetables. Next, one of James' boyhood dreams comes true as he has the ultimate falconry experience.

programme.title: James Martin's Great British Adventure
programme.desc: James Martin's British adventure travels as far to the east of England as this journey will go, to Norfolk, where he is joined by his good old mate and top local chef, Galton Blackiston. They meet in Cromer, where Galton has a successful fish and chip shop, which inspires James to cook pan-fried cod with a warm tartar sauce on the beach. Then James heads to Norfolk Lavender to find out why it grows so well in this county and how it's distilled for oil.

programme.title: James Martin's Great British Adventure
programme.desc: No adventure around the UK would be complete without a trip to the capital city, London. James is checking out some of his old haunts and stopping in on Clare Smyth's brand new restaurant Core for a bite to eat and a catch up with the world's best female chef. Then high up on the rooftops of London, he cooks up spiced pigeon breast with dhal. Next, James ventures into south east London to a knife forgery in Peckham. He gets hands on in the process of making Japanese style knives.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri visits America's most interesting and unique restaurants. Guy finds fresh crab in San Francisco, homemade granola in Nevada, and boiled turkey in North Carolina.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Tom Parker is eager to impress with a salad that includes 10 elements. Meanwhile, Paul Askew, hopes his traditional techniques will triumph over his younger competitors, and Ellis Barrie is taking a risk with a creative cucumber and oyster starter.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is the fish course and the atmosphere in the kitchen gets even tenser as the three newcomers begin to understand the perfection and skill the veteran chef demands. Ellis Barrie attempts to create the flavour of a summer barbecue.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is the main course and the pressure is intense in the kitchen. In the hope of impressing the veteran judge, Paul Askew is serving an extremely personal main course based on his mother's favourite food.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Dessert is the last chance for the chefs to secure a place cooking for the judges and the pressure in the kitchen is intense. Paul can't afford to make any mistakes and is pushing himself with a technically challenging baked alaska.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining North West chefs must cook their entire taste of summer menus again. The guest judge is Greg Rusedski, former British number one, who understands the high standards expected at Wimbledon.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Returning banquet champion Tommy Banks takes on two ambitious newcomers Danny Parker and Josh Overington. They start by finding out whois the surprise veteran judge and who will be marking their dishes.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The atmosphere in the kitchen gets even more heated, the three chefs are cooking their fish courses. Josh Overington, creates a decadent dish inspired by the prestige of the Wimbledon Championships.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs are cooking their main courses and are all taking risks to try and win a place at the banquet. Danny Parker, is trying to recreate the flavours of an American barbecue but the veteran chef is dubious about one of his ingredients.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Dessert is the last chance for the chefs to secure a place cooking for the judges and the pressure in the kitchen is intense. All the chefs are using unusual ingredients in their desserts.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining chefs from the North East must cook their entire taste of summer menus again. They're hoping to impress the formidable panel of judges Oliver Peyton, Matthew Fort, Andi Oliver, and guest Judy Murray.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is the Scottish regional heats and returning chefs to the competition Michael Bremner and Ally McGrath battle ambitious newcomer Angela Malik for a place in the national finals. This time they are cooking their starters.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The atmosphere in the kitchen gets even more heated and the veteran chef is expecting perfection from the fish course. Ally McGrath creates an inventive dish based on his summer memories of rock pooling in the River Tweed.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Angela Malik is in her element, cooking a tiffin box based on memories of summer picnics. Michael Bremner is taking a huge risk, and Ally is creating an inventive dish based on a summer barbeque.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Dessert is the last chance for the chefs to secure a place cooking for the judges and the pressure in the kitchen is intense. Angela Malik is cooking a technically challenging dessert.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two Scottish chefs must cook their entire taste of summer menus again. They're hoping to impress the formidable panel of judges Oliver Peyton, Matthew Fort and Andi Oliver. They are joined by guest judge Gary Parsons.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Determined newcomers Paul Croasdale and Nick Brodie take on returning chef Phil Carmichael for a place in the national finals. It's Phil's third time in the competition and this year he's determined to make it to the banquet.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The pressure in the kitchen is high as the chefs prepare their fish courses. Paul Croasdale is trying to recreate the elegance of Wimbledon with luxury ingredients such as king crab and scallops.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It's the main course and all three chefs are cooking Welsh lamb in hope of impressing. Phil is taking a risk with barbecued lamb and in an attempt to elevate a humble barbecue to banquet level, he's serving it with many accompaniments.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It's the dessert course and the last chance for the chefs to secure a place cooking for the judges so all three chefs are feeling the pressure. Phil Carmichael creates a tennis ball dessert that combines two British classics, Eton mess and trifle.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two winning chefs from the Wales region must cook their entire taste of summer menus again. They're hoping to impress the formidable panel of judges and guest judge Dan Bloxham, Wimbledon's Master of Ceremonies.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: With two spots left in the National Finals, it's the turn of the Central chefs to battle it out. Pip Lacey, head chef at the Michelin starred Murano in London, tried her luck in the competition two years ago. She's takes on two newcomers.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: With the chefs hoping to impress, they each take risks. Pip Lacey combines lobster with strawberry in a tribute to strawberries and cream. Ryan Simpson's also using lobster in his recreation of a BBQ and has invented a technique to create his chips.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Today the chefs cook their main courses and have very different takes on the Taste of Summer brief. Ryan Simpson hopes to elevate a ploughman's lunch into a dish worthy of the banquet, and Nick Deverell-Smith celebrates a meat he thinks is underused.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Dessert is the last chance for the chefs to secure a place cooking for the judges. Pip Lacey's pineapple dessert uses coriander. Nick Deverell-Smith also pays tribute to the pineapple, creating a dessert featuring a rice pudding made with cous-cous.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two chefs who've made it through cook their four courses again. They must impress the formidable panel of judges Oliver Peyton, Matthew Fort, Andi Oliver and guest judge Jordanne Whiley, one of Britain's most successful tennis players.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs meet their surprise veteran judge and cook their starters. Eddie is hoping to do better than last year, Joery is hoping to impress with a tribute to Richard Krajicek, and Tommy is creating a warm salad.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Joery Castel is looking to triumph by combing mackerel and cucumber. Tommy Heaney hopes his Murray Mound is a fitting tribute to Andi Murray, Eddie Attwell is creating five different strawberry elements to accompany the scallops in his dish.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Tommy Heaney's BBQ dish is a surf and turf combination, Joery Castel's aiming to recreate a more American style BBQ and Eddie Attwell's taking a risk with an unusual meat.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Tommy Heaney's BBQ dish is a surf and turf combination, Joery Castel's aiming to recreate a more American style BBQ and Eddie Attwell's taking a risk with an unusual meat.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two chefs who've made it through cook their four courses again. They're hoping to impress the formidable panel of judges Oliver Peyton, Matthew Fort and Andi Oliver who are joined this week by tennis' greatest entertainer, Mansour Bahrami.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: With Finals Week ahead of them, judge Andi Oliver first summons the chefs to Wimbledon to take a look at where they could be cooking if they win. The judges are joined by guest judge Sue Barker, host of the Wimbledon coverage for more than 20 years.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs must cook their fish courses but with some exceptionally high scoring dishes in the regional heats, each chef will need to deliver perfection in order to see off the competition. The judges are joined by guest judge Tim Henman.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is main course day and with only two spots remaining on the banquet menu, the competition is fierce. As the barbecues are fired up, all 8 chefs are feeling the heat. The judges are joined by guest judgeand Paralympic gold medallist, Gordon Reid.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is dessert day and with just one place remaining at the Wimbledon banquet, each chef is giving everything they have got. The judges are joined by guest judge Annabel Croft, former British Number One and Junior Wimbledon Champion.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It's the grand finale and the winning chefs must now cook their dishes at the banquet. Once in the kitchen, the chefs face the huge challenge of scaling up their dishes.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Competing for top spot are Danny Parker, head chef at Michelin starred `House of Tides' in Newcastle, who is back to try his luck in the competition again after being knocked out in the regional heat last year following a tense tie-break. He's taking on two newcomers: Dan Fletcher, protégé of Phil Howard, and now head chef at `Fenchurch' in the City of London, and David Coulson,who runs `Peace and Loaf' in Newcastle, which boasts 2 AA rosettes.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: After a tense starter course the chefs are all aiming for 10s with their unique fish dishes. David is celebrating a personal connection to the brief with his `My Cod I Bet Me Ma'am Has Seen Some Trotters', a tribute to his mum who has worked as a chiropodist. Dan's `For All Those Sleepless Nights' pays tribute to nightshift workers from the NHS with some bold but classic flavours while Danny's `Not All Superheroes Wear Capes' aims to give a taste of luxury to the hardworking staff.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Dan pays tribute to an icon of modern nursing, Florence Nightingale, while Danny in inspired by the founder of the NHS, Aneurin Bevan. David's dish is dedicated to the air ambulance staff who saved the life of his fiancé, and he even takes a trip to meet one of them.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It's the chefs' last chance to impress and tensions are high. Dan takes a risk with an unusual combination of ewe's milk and bergamot while Danny is determined to pull out all the stops with his complex dessert `Aunty Gwen's Chocolate'. David hopes to transform a childhood comfort classic into a show stopping dessert. Dan also takes a trip to meet Dr Ben Clarke who was working in the Royal London Hospital A&E during the London Bridge terror attack.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Finds include the lost WWI training trenches on Salisbury Plain, Britain's first `double henge', discovered just down the road from Stonehenge, where the evidence suggests our ancestors feasted and made sacred offerings as part of a visit to the ritualistic Stonehenge landscape, and luxury foreign goods discovered at Tintagel, the legendary childhood home of King Arthur.

programme.title: Secrets of Great British Castles

programme.title: Secrets of Great British Castles
programme.desc: Built by William the Conqueror, the Tower of London is one of Britain's most iconic landmarks. It has been a military fortress, a palace, a royal mint, a prison, a zoo and a place of execution, a silent witness to some of the most momentous events in our history. Dan Jones reveals some of its fascinating secrets and tells the stories of the traitors, rebels and royalty who met their end inside its imposing walls.

programme.title: Al Murray: Why Does Everyone Hate the British Empire?
programme.desc: Al Murray pairs up with local comedians to explore the history of British colonial rule and its legacy. They find out about the origins of British rule, its impact and how its legacy continues to influence governance, culture, and attitudes.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A two-bedroom detached house in Basford in Nottingham, a top floor flat in Portsmouth and a large Edwardian terrace in Crewe are all sold under the hammer. Martin, Lucy and Dion meet the new owners and hear about their plans.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A multimillion-pound development in Farningham in Kent, an ambitious, total renovation project in Aspatria in Cumbria and a very small studio flat in Wallington in Surrey are all sold under the hammer. Martin and Lucy meet the new owners to discuss their plans.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Lucy meets a retired bingo caller who buys a renovation project in Maidstone, and Martin has to navigate his way through a maze of a building to find a one-bedroom flat in Salford and calls in on a house in Stoke with an outside loo.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Lucy looks round a rundown two-bedroom flat in Fulham, and Martin visits a former railway cottage in the North East and a terraced house near the Kent coast that needs doing up.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin and Lucy visit a holiday home on the south coast, a detached house in the Midlands and a great tenement flat with much potential in Glasgow.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin visits a two-bedroom house in Stoke with wonderful countryside views and a great ground floor flat in Glasgow, while Lucy meets two boyhood friends who join forces to buy and renovate a house in north London.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Lucy Alexander tours a farmhouse and 10-acre equestrian centre in Kent, and Martin Roberts is in the midlands and the north looking at more modest renovation projects.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts and Lucy Alexander visit three houses near water in different parts of the country, including a wonderful cottage with a stunning view in Devon, a town house in Sheerness and a charming one-bedroom cottage in North Shields.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: It is a family affair as a father and daughter, a mum and her son and two cousins tackle very different renovation projects, from an end terrace to a plot of land.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Lucy Alexander is serenaded by an opera singer who property develops in his spare time, and Martin Roberts is in the Midlands and Cumbria meeting a couple and a young man, both determined to make money from their property investments.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Lucy meets three friends who give a top floor flat in Harrow a high-end finish, and Martin meets a seasoned property developer in Durham and a PhD student in Stoke on Trent who both hope to make money on their three bedroom property investments.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Lucy has a look around a former funeral directors in Maidstone and a two-bedroom purpose built flat in Cricklewood, while Martin catches up with a young man who took up property developing in Stoke on Trent after a motorcycle accident.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts looks around a semi in Northern Ireland and a flat in Newcastle, while Lucy Alexander has a look at a basement flat in Dover which boasts a stunning view.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Lucy Alexander meets a couple who buy an old prefab in the New Forest and hope to knock it down and build their dream home. Martin Roberts meets a man who breaks a property developers' rule and buys a flat on an impulse.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Lucy Alexander looks at a derelict house in Surrey where the new owners plan to build four houses, and Martin Roberts meets a novice developer in Crewe and a couple of seasoned professionals in Cheshire.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts meets plumber Amy who does a fantastic job renovating a cottage in a Cornish village. Also, two sets of farmers, in Cumbria and Northern Ireland, both hoping to make money on property developing projects.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin and Lucy travel the country from the South East to the North East, dropping in on Northern Ireland too. The properties are just as different as the regions from a bungalow and a terraced house to a unique water tower.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin meets a father and son building team working on a large semi in Derbyshire and a mother and daughter in Belfast doing up a four-bed terrace, and Lucy looks around a more modest one-bedroom flat in Clydebank in Scotland.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin and Lucy look around two terraced houses and a large semi in the Midlands and the North East. There's a rush on with one of the projects - a couple want to turn their auction lot into the perfect family home before their second baby arrives.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin and Lucy see two very different terraced houses in Lynemouth and Belfast and a bungalow in Derby which a couple plan to make their forever home.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Lucy visits a former travel agency in Folkestone in Kent, Martin follows the work of renovating a rundown community centre in Dunfermline in Scotland, and we meet a family that are fixing up a fire damaged terrace in Carlisle.

programme.desc: A retired navy man and his family renovate a flat in Port Glasgow while developers in Derbyshire and Kent try and make money on their terraced houses.

programme.title: Tonight
programme.sub-title: Over the Counter: Our Hidden Drug Addiction?
programme.desc: GPs and pharmacists are raising concerns that drugs including codeine, oxymetazoline and antihistamines present significant dangers of becoming habit-forming, even addictive if not used correctly. Sam Holder examines a hidden dependence that can devastate lives and cause long-term health problems. He meets those struggling to come off drugs that can be bought in any high street pharmacy without a prescription, and we hear from specialists who warn of what can happen in extreme cases of overuse.

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: The story of everyday life in a small and tightly-knit community - this is the core of the world's longest running prime-time drama serial and the highest-rating programme in the UK. Coronation Street has for over 50 years uniquely combined cult status with popularity and durability - the quality of writing is second to none, portraying believable characters with a large helping of humour.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: When a farmer is found horribly murdered in the middle of a stone circle, suspicion points at a group of druids. DCI John Barnaby and DS Jones hurry to the scene of the crime, where they are joined by new pathologist Kate Wilding on her first assignment. Druid high priest Leticia Clifford shows Barnaby that sacred ley lines run through the stone circle and later is convinced she has made an exciting discovery.

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: The story of everyday life in a small and tightly-knit community - this is the core of the world's longest running prime-time drama serial and the highest-rating programme in the UK. Coronation Street has for over 50 years uniquely combined cult status with popularity and durability - the quality of writing is second to none, portraying believable characters with a large helping of humour.

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: The story of everyday life in a small and tightly-knit community - this is the core of the world's longest running prime-time drama serial and the highest-rating programme in the UK. Coronation Street has for over 50 years uniquely combined cult status with popularity and durability - the quality of writing is second to none, portraying believable characters with a large helping of humour.

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Police investigate a series of mysterious events that appear to be a result of witchcraft, and Greengrass becomes convinced the hidden properties of local mud will make his fortune.

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Emotions run high as police investigate the kidnap of a baby, and Greengrass turns to inexperienced hands to help him concrete a drive.

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: When a woman is found killed in a remote cottage in the dramatic Northumberland countryside, DI Vera Stanhope is taken back to a place full of childhood memories - and an unsolved case from her own past. She and her team delve into the local politics surrounding a controversial proposal to dig a new quarry, and find motives for murder in the unlikeliest of places, but the truth could be hidden within the land itself.

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Follow on from the `Inspector Morse' series.

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: When a woman is found dead in a hotel room, the personal ads on a website look like they might hold the key, and while Rachel enjoys her secret relationship with Will, Janet investigates speed dating.

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: When an unconscious baby is admitted to hospital, it's unclear who had his best interests at heart, and while Rachel is stung by Gill's harsh words, Janet feels lost when Elise decides to leave home.

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: The inspector reduces the canteen's hours and fires the canteen girl, prompting Stan to a one man hunger strike.

programme.title: No Room for Error
programme.desc: The Isle of Man is in the throes of a fortnight like no other - the three-wheelers are moving heaven and earth to achieve their dreams and flinging themselves from apex to apex to break lap records. From brothers in arms honing machinery to thrills and spills, there's pelvic pain and medical hurdles to overcome - however, the sidecar roster is a hardy breed of men and women who stare fear in the eye and let fear blink first.

programme.title: The Protectors
programme.desc: The Protectors guard a bank robber's corpse.

programme.title: BattleBots
programme.desc: New crowd favourite Ripperoni offers a slice of its deadly pizza weapon to HiJinx, and Malice is determined to carve a victory against Valkyrie - meanwhile, the world's best flipper Hydra tries to finally hit the roof with Free Shipping. Elsewhere, it's a family affair when Blip meets its stablemate Tantrum - both bots are now desperate for a win after a poor start, and the gloves are off even though they have the same designer.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: enry Cole and Simon O'Brien head to Redditch where Henry restores a 70s Kawasaki motorbike, while Simon takes a pair of rusty cinema seat supports and creates a desirable garden bench.

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: The boys are coming to the end of their journey as they head to Wales where Henry converts a Royal Enfield into a classic café racer, and Simon gets an 80s pinball machine back to its best.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Simon White and friend and neighbour Shirley Boyle are first to host at The Old Hall Country House in the picturesque village of Madeley in Cheshire. On arrival most of the guests are awed by the historical 16th century B&B. In the afternoon, Simon and Shirley take their guests tap dancing and in keeping with the five-star luxury to a private fine dining restaurant for dinner.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: It's day two in the competition and the turn of cat-lover Eileen Finn to cater for her B&B competitors at the four-star Thornley House in Allendale, Northumbria. After being blown away by first hosts Simon and Shirley at the luxury Old Hall Country House, Eileen has her work cut out. Also, Eileen's beloved and very large Maine Coon cats are not to all of her guests' liking.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Jessica and Adrian Rashleigh are third to host at their three bedroom establishment Chy Conyn in St Ives, on the beautiful Cornish coast. The hosts hope that what they lack for in size they will make up for in stunning surroundings. For the activity Jessica and Adrian take their guests on a magical mystery camper van tour of the local sights. At breakfast some of the guests struggle with the small breakfast room.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final hosts are Tim and Karen from The Cavendish in Bournemouth. The guests are initially impressed with the 16-bed `pink palace', but things go downhill when Simon and Shirley vigorously test their bed - and break it. Jessica and Adrian are worried that their room, situated right next to the front door, may mean a noisy night. Eileen however is delighted by the feline memorabilia left in her room as a special touch.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The rivals reconvene for one last time to find out how much they've been paid by their fellow B&B owners, and to sort out their differences. Simon White of Old Hall Country House is stunned by a generous overpayment, but Eileen Finn from the four-star Thornley is on the receiving end of some harsh criticism and some even harsher payments, and a shock underpayment leaves Tim from The Cavendish in Bournemouth reeling.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The competition is in Exeter this time and first to host is 35-year-old salon owner Hannah, who's hoping to impress her guests with a menu bursting with taste sensations.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: It's day two of the competition in Exeter and funeral director and BMX fanatic Simon is hoping an evening of big portions and `man' food will bag him the top spot.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: It's the third day of the competition in Exeter and the turn of Pascale to host. Pascale prepares a Vietnamese menu for her guests, full of flavour and colour.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: It's the fourth day of the competition in Exeter and the turn of Nick to host. Nick prepares a menu that represents his personality: big, bold and brash.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Exeter, Estate agent Ajay is hoping her nautical themed evening will see her sail to victory. Along with a gigantic seafood spread, Ajay's also laid on a boat trip around Torquay harbour.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition kicks off at The King's Head in Addlethorpe, with landlady Helen Coy and her bar manager and best buddy Emma Green pulling out all the stops to win.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit of the week is to dog friendly Beck Hall in North Yorkshire, run by B&B newcomers Louise and Andy Macbeth.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit of the week is to The Albaston in Torquay, owned by avid souvenir collectors Kevin and Julie Shadbolt.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final visit of the week is to a specialist dance holiday venue, the Hotel Piccadilly, in Bournemouth, where owners Terri and Stewart Flett hope to get their guests in a spin.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: It's the final day, and the owners of The King's Head, Beck Hall, The Albaston and the Hotel Piccadilly are meeting for the last time to find out what they've been paid and to settle some scores.

programme.desc: For four seasons fans of `Teen Mom' watched Farrah, Maci, Amber and Catelynn face multiple challenges in their first years of motherhood, including such things as graduating from high school, relationships, getting jobs, beginning college and moving out on their own. Oh, and raising their first child. Now the original girls - hence, the `OG' in the revived series title - return to reveal the challenges of raising children on camera, and how life in the public eye has shaped them as parents and people. It's an unprecedented view of young mothers facing the responsibilities of parenthood as their children enter kindergarten, start little league and reach other milestones of youth.

programme.desc: The day of Mrs. Cow and Mr. Bull wedding arrives, but not everything goes to plan; nothing stops it being the best wedding ever and there is a lot of happy crying.

programme.title: Peppa Pig

programme.desc: Keys has promised a new song to Radio Wyndley but hasn't left herself much time to write it. She's low on ideas, and she visits her neighbours for inspiration. Duster dog, Isabella the bear and little poet Caylee provide her with too many ideas.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants

programme.title: Tales of the Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles
programme.desc: To save his brothers, Mikey needs the help of Bishop's intern, Rod; but Rod wants something in return; he wants to be turned into a mutant.

programme.title: Sonic Prime

programme.title: Tales of the Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles

programme.title: Wylde Pak

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole, Simon O'Brien and Max McMurdo travel across the UK to find unique items lost in people's homes to fix up for a profit. This episode features a Raleigh shopper bike.

programme.title: The Yorkshire Auction House
programme.desc: Auctioneer Angus Ashworth and his team perform house clearances and uncover hidden gems. What will these antiques sell for at auction?

programme.title: The Yorkshire Auction House
programme.desc: Auctioneer Angus Ashworth and his team are called out to people's homes with a mission to sift through a lifetime of possessions and find forgotten treasures that can be sold at auction.

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: It's last time out for experts Roo Irvine and Angus Ashworth, shopping in the west country for their final auction. Roo enjoys the fun of the fair while Angus enjoys a tipple.

programme.title: The Yorkshire Auction House
programme.sub-title: Two Hundred Clocks One Rare Vase
programme.desc: Auctioneer Angus Ashworth and his team are called out to people's homes with a mission to sift through a lifetime of possessions and find forgotten treasures that can be sold at auction.

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team restore a miniature house, a Bakelite radio, a fairground game and an Indian painting.

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team restore an iconic 1960s armchair, a Victorian electrostatic generator, a WWII sailor's hat and the most extraordinary bicycle.

programme.title: How Do They Do It?
programme.desc: How do they cool Sydney Opera House, turn Sheffield steel into a carpenter's handsaw, make a microphone that can pick up a whisper, and steer a safe course through a frozen river?

programme.title: How Do They Do It?
programme.desc: How do they brew moonshine in the heart of New York City, make a battery powered bicycle, and grow the rubber for wetsuits in the Arizona desert?

programme.title: How Do They Do It?
programme.desc: How do they mine one of the rarest gemstones in the world, craft the classic cowboy boot, and build a monster road sweeper that can pick up 7 tonnes of trash?

programme.title: How Do They Do It?
programme.desc: How do they make the world's softest duvets, drill for oil thousands of metres below ground, and put the stretch in the world's stretchiest rubber bands?

programme.title: Wheeler Dealers
programme.desc: The Golf GTi was a symbol of the 80s and gave birth to the term "hot hatch"; Mike finds a prime example of a Rabbit which he believes can be transformed; the market is crying out for cars like this, but Ant has a lot of work to do.

programme.title: Wheeler Dealers
programme.desc: The guys are excited when they find a legendary 1991 Toyota MR2 Turbo. But, one small fix becomes a big problem for Ant as the entire engine has to be removed.

programme.title: Shed & Buried
programme.desc: Motorcycle fanatic Henry Cole travels around the country with his mechanic mate Fuzz Townshend nosing around people's sheds and garages in the hope of finding some automotive bargains.

programme.title: Shed & Buried
programme.desc: In the first episode, they travel from Cornwall to Oxfordshire to rummage around in some of Britain's most amazing sheds in search of a nice little earner. Their discoveries include vintage tractors, engines and a clutch of historic motorbikes.

programme.title: How It's Made
programme.sub-title: Headphones; Diving Regulators; Reflector Light Bulbs
programme.desc: More everyday items are put under the microscope as we discover how headphones, diving regulators and reflector light bulbs are made.

programme.title: How It's Made

programme.title: Say Yes to the Dress UK
programme.desc: Keturah has her heart set on a fishtail gown, but her brother suggests a princess look. Meanwhile, Karen's dreams for a ball gown may not come true.

programme.title: Say Yes to the Dress
programme.desc: Lori has kept a secret from her entourage, and now it's time for the big reveal; merchandise Director Dorothy tries on a wedding dress for the first time.

programme.title: Say Yes to the Dress
programme.desc: Non-binary bride Kevin has a vision that tests Randy and Peter; new consultant Olivia struggles to hold her own against the world's bossiest best friend.

programme.title: Disappeared
programme.desc: Michelle disappears, and soon afterwards her abandoned car is found. Has she been abducted, or is she on the run from the law?

programme.title: Judge Rinder's Crime Stories
programme.desc: A career woman finds herself the target of an elaborate con, and a domestic abuse victim enlists the help of a hypnotherapist to bring her attacker to justice.

programme.title: Diabolical: Deadly Love
programme.desc: In 1980, two eerily similar crimes occur 400 miles apart. They both leave a young mother dead and an infant child stolen. Years later, police unravel a shocking plot.

programme.title: Disappeared
programme.desc: Eight-year old Zachary Bernhardt disappears from his Florida home late at night while his mum is out. When another boy goes missing, new theories emerge.

programme.title: Murder on CCTV
programme.desc: In 2014, graduate student Tequila Suter is stabbed to death in her suburban Buffalo home; investigators' only hope of solving the case is to piece together a trail of CCTV across town and follow in the footsteps of a killer.

programme.title: The Killer In My Family
programme.desc: Ian Huntley's former girlfriend and daughter tell their side of the story. The pair explain how Huntley abused them for years, before they eventually ended all contact with him.

programme.title: American Monster
programme.desc: Christy Blankenship is stolen from her family on what should be a joyous day, when police find her dead at home, with a gunshot wound to her head. Her husband insists it's a terrible accident. But only that morning he had vowed 'til death do us part.

programme.title: American Monster
programme.desc: Matt Hendershot's wife cheats on him with his friend, devastating him. Following the death of her lover, he allows her back into his home, along with a collection of the dead man's guns. Little does she know, it is a mistake that will prove fatal.

programme.title: American Detective With Lt. Joe Kenda
programme.desc: In Northern California, police find an accomplished US Forest Service worker executed beneath a mound of snow. A missing wallet and a pair of jumper cables send Greg Hagwood on a multi-state manhunt for a deranged killer who knows no bounds.

programme.title: Judge Rinder's Crime Stories
programme.desc: The grave story of a woman who was set on fire in her own home and the hunt for the suspect, her brother. A series of thefts of laughing gas canisters from hospitals around the country lead to a police car pursuit.

programme.title: Murder on CCTV
programme.desc: Christian Aguilar mysteriously vanishes from his university campus. Police are stumped, until some CCTV footage leads the case in a shocking new direction.

programme.title: Lethally Blonde
programme.desc: The team looks into tragic stories ripped from the headlines, exposing the dark side of fame.

programme.title: Evil Lives Here
programme.desc: At the age of sixteen, Roxie rushes into marriage. Her husband manipulates and threatens her nearly every day. After 30 years in an abusive marriage, Roxie finally leaves her husband. But he makes her pay a terrible price for that decision.

programme.title: Murder on CCTV
programme.desc: When loving couple Gary and Linda Haas fail to reach their holiday campsite, New Mexico State Police are called in to investigate. With no forensic evidence and no witnesses, all they can do is scour CCTV footage for clues to unravel the mystery.

programme.title: Murder on CCTV
programme.desc: When Marcel Reardon is discovered bludgeoned to death outside an apartment building, his friends deny all knowledge of his murder; detectives are convinced at least one of them is lying.

programme.title: Forensic Detectives
programme.desc: Detectives use the remains of bones and teeth to identify murder victims.

programme.title: Murder on CCTV
programme.desc: In 2013, a shopkeeper is shot dead in his store in Columbus, Ohio. With the shooting caught on camera and the killers on the run, detectives rush to catch them.

programme.title: Murder on CCTV
programme.desc: A family's nightmare begins in September 2014 when 8-year-old Hiawayi Robinson leaves her apartment to play but never returns; the desperate search to find her leads investigators to surveillance footage that sends shockwaves through the community.

programme.title: When Fred Met Rose
programme.desc: Documentary series examining what led Fred and Rose West to become serial killers.

programme.title: Fred and Rose: The West Murders
programme.desc: Focuses on the notorious house which the couple shared in Gloucester. Fred and Rose murdered their first three victims - Anna McFall, Charmaine West and then Rena Costello - because they `got in the way'. But when they moved into 25 Cromwell Street, the couple began to kill for sexual thrills.

programme.title: Fred and Rose: The West Murders
programme.desc: Looks at how a dogged police officer, Hazel Savage, fought to have the Wests' patio excavated when she became suspicious about the whereabouts of their daughter. The film also includes insights from both Fred and Rose West's former solicitors.

programme.title: Mafia's Greatest Hits
programme.desc: Gambino family soldier Roy DeMeo and his crew were suspected of killing dozens of people and dismembering their bodies, but the FBI and other mobsters were soon on his trail.

programme.title: Mafia's Greatest Hits
programme.desc: Meyer Lansky was an associate of `Lucky' Luciano and linked to criminal financial operations, but the FBI struggled to pin anything on him - or find his riches.

programme.title: Mafia's Greatest Hits
programme.desc: The final episode of this gripping series reveals what happened when the FBI pursued the five clans who ran organised crime in New York, as well as the fate of two brave informers.

programme.title: Trevor McDonald and the Killer Nurse
programme.desc: Trevor McDonald investigates the case of serial child killer Beverley Allitt and reflects on how it was reported at the time. A combination of archive from the time, interviews with victims and expert analysis will piece together this tragic and shocking story.

programme.title: Wayne Couzens: Killer in Plain Sight
programme.desc: This extraordinary film reveals the shocking truth about the serving police officer who killed Sarah Everard, and asks how he was able to hide from scrutiny for so long.

programme.title: Surviving Sinaga
programme.desc: Reynhard Sinaga is the UK's most prolific rapist. Meeting men outside Manchester nightclubs, he drugged, sexually assaulted and filmed his depraved acts with at least 200 young men for the past 12 years. Featuring interviews with those affected.

programme.title: Heartland Homicide
programme.desc: In a tiny hamlet just outside the town of Bonnyville, Alberta, Canada, 58-year-old Doreen Bradley, owner and operator of a successful fast food restaurant, is found murdered in her rural home.

programme.title: Heartland Homicide
programme.desc: A fire engulfs the home of the Klaus family in the middle the night while everyone is asleep, and the investigators suspect that it isn't an accident. The whole case changes when the son starts seeing the spirits of his dead family.

programme.title: Heartland Homicide

programme.title: Novocaine
programme.desc: When the girl of his dreams gets kidnapped, a man turns his inability to feel pain into an unexpected advantage as he fights a bunch of thugs to get her back.

programme.title: The Cinema List: Remakes

programme.title: 300: Rise of an Empire
programme.desc: Greek Gen. Themistocles leads the charge to fight invading Persian forces led by the god Xerxes -- once a mortal -- and Artemisia.

programme.title: Nobody 2
programme.desc: Workaholic assassin Hutch Mansell takes his family on a much-needed holiday to the small tourist town of Plummerville. However, he soon finds himself in the crosshairs of a corrupt theme-park operator, a shady sheriff, and a bloodthirsty crime boss.

programme.title: Clash of the Titans
programme.desc: Perseus travels the underworld to defeat the evil god Hades, who seeks to overthrow Zeus and bring hell to Earth.

programme.title: Spider-Man
programme.desc: Adaptation of the adventures of the swinging comic-strip hero. A shy student becomes a crime fighter after a bite from a mutant spider, during a school outing, gives him extraordinary powers. As he comes to terms with his new-found abilities, he is also forced to contend with a maniac villain known as the Green Goblin.

programme.title: Gladiator II
programme.desc: Lucius must enter the Colosseum after the powerful emperors of Rome conquer his home. With rage in his heart and the future of the empire at stake, he looks to the past to find the strength and honour needed to return the glory of Rome to its people.

programme.title: Twisters
programme.desc: Competing teams of storm chasers find themselves in a fight for their lives as multiple systems converge over central Oklahoma.

programme.title: Morbius
programme.desc: Biochemist Michael Morbius tries to cure himself of a rare blood disease, but when his experiment goes wrong, he inadvertently infects himself with a form of vampirism instead.

programme.title: Spider-Man 2
programme.desc: The comic book web-slinger returns for a blockbusting sequel. Crime-fighting has cost Peter Parker his best friend and his true love Mary Jane. However, just as he quits as Spider-Man, New York comes under threat from Dr Octopus, a metal-tentacled madman. Peter has no choice but to swing back into action.

programme.title: Olympus Has Fallen
programme.desc: America's national security team must rely on a disgraced former guard to save the president after terrorists seize control of the White House, taking the chief of state hostage.

programme.title: Fuze
programme.desc: London undergoes a citywide evacuation when a World War II bomb is unearthed at a construction site. Amid the escalating chaos, a daring criminal operation is set in motion -- one that uses the evacuation as a cover for a meticulously planned heist.

programme.title: Enter the Dragon
programme.desc: A kung fu expert is assigned to penetrate an island fortress to destroy an opium and white slavery empire.

programme.title: Prisoner of War
programme.desc: Captured by Japanese forces during World War II, a British SAS officer and his fellow prisoners are forced to fight in brutal death matches for the entertainment of their captors.

programme.title: Sky Cinema Preview
programme.desc: Previews of the great films now playing on Sky Cinema, plus other highlights showing on Sky.

programme.title: Am I OK?
programme.desc: When Lucy realises that she's never been attracted to men, her lifelong friend Jane helps her navigate coming out in her 30s.

programme.title: Honey Don't!
programme.desc: Small-town private investigator Honey O'Donahue probes a series of strange deaths that are tied to a mysterious church.

programme.title: Roofman
programme.desc: Escaped convict Jeffrey Manchester hides out in a Toys "R" Us, surviving undetected for months while planning his next move. However, his double life starts to unravel when he falls for a divorced mum, setting off a compelling game of cat and mouse.

programme.title: Spinal Tap II: The End Continues
programme.desc: Documentarian Marty DiBergi follows Spinal Tap as they prepare for a reunion concert. Joined by Paul McCartney and Elton John, they wrestle with their chequered past to put on a show that they hope will solidify their place in rock 'n' roll history.

programme.title: Sacramento
programme.desc: Rickey, an energetic and free-spirited young man with a Peter Pan complex, convinces his long-time friend Glenn to hit pause on his blissful domestic life and embark on an impromptu road trip across California.

programme.title: Little Fockers
programme.desc: Greg and Pam have been together for ten years, and now they're the proud parents of two adorable twins. However, money is tight and in order to keep his family afloat, devoted nurse Greg takes a second job working for a drug company. That development doesn't sit well with Jack, whose previous suspicions regarding his hapless son-in-law soon come back in full force after learning of this development.

programme.title: Finding Your Feet
programme.desc: A middle-class snob on the eve of retirement takes refuge with her bohemian sister after she discovers her husband is having an affair with her best friend.

programme.title: The Tuxedo
programme.desc: An ordinary cabbie uses gadget-laden formal wear to combat a villain who wants to poison the world's water supply.

programme.title: Mean Machine
programme.desc: A footballer is jailed for throwing a crucial game. Whilst inside, the football-loving warden sets him the task of coaching a team of prisoners to take on the all-conquering warders' side. Can he form a disparate bunch of prisoners into a winning team?

programme.title: Just Go With It
programme.desc: His heart recently broken, plastic surgeon Danny Maccabee (Adam Sandler) pretends to be married so he can enjoy future dates with no strings attached. His web of lies works, but when he meets Palmer (Brooklyn Decker) -- the gal of his dreams -- she resists involvement. Instead of coming clean, Danny enlists Katherine (Jennifer Aniston), his assistant, to pose as his soon-to-be-ex-wife. Instead of solving Danny's problems, the lies create more trouble.

programme.title: About Last Night ...
programme.desc: Two Chicago singles try to build a more meaningful relationship with each other following a one-night stand.

programme.desc: Two fathers get laid off from their well-paid jobs and are forced to stay at home to look after their kids. Seeing the opportunity to make money, the two of them open their own day-care centre. With very little experience and some fairly unconventional ideas on child care, can they really hope to compete against the prim and proper Chapman Academy, which has monopolised the child care industry in their town?

programme.title: Pets on a Train
programme.desc: When a crew of animal bandits embark on a routine swindle, they find themselves caught up in a train heist. It's up to Falcon, a petty thief raccoon, and Rex, a righteous police-dog, to save the animals on this high-speed runway train.

programme.title: Paddington in Peru
programme.desc: Paddington and his adopted family, the Browns, decide to visit his aunt Lucy in Peru, but circumstances lead them towards misadventures in the Amazon rainforest and mountains of Peru.

programme.title: Penguins of Madagascar
programme.desc: The plucky penguins join a special inter-species task force in an attempt to stop an evil doctor from taking over the world.

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog 3
programme.desc: Sonic, Knuckles and Tails reunite to battle Shadow, a mysterious new enemy with powers unlike anything they've faced before. With their abilities outmatched in every way, they seek out an unlikely alliance to stop Shadow and protect the planet.

programme.desc: Visually exuberant tale about a neurotic ant who struggles to express his individuality in a colony of conformists. He attempts to break from his totalitarian society while at the same time trying to win the affection of the princess he loves.

programme.desc: Slapstick tale which follows the misfortunes of two hard-up brothers whose plans to renovate and sell a valuable building are jeopardised by a rogue mouse. The brothers soon find themselves pulling out all the stops in order to evict their tiny resident.

programme.desc: A group of New York tenement dwellers face eviction and receive help from an unlikely source. Just when it seems that nothing short of a miracle will save their homes, a miracle is just what they get in the shape of tiny visitors from outer space whose magical powers have the bad guys on the run.

programme.desc: Based on the Mary Norton novels about a four-inch-tall family who live beneath the floorboards of a human house. The family's future is threatened when the house falls into the hands of an evil lawyer who plans to destroy it.

programme.desc: From page to screen, the Cinema List celebrates book adaptations. Featuring Wuthering Heights starring Margot Robbie and Bridget Jones's Diary, see these stories on film.

programme.title: Ghostbusters
programme.desc: Supernatural comedy about three New York parapsychology professors who decide to set up a ghost-catching agency. Soon a storm of malevolent spirits threatens to destroy the city, and the mysterious contents of a woman's fridge suggest a paranormal Armageddon.

programme.title: Ghostbusters II
programme.desc: The phantom-fighters return to the streets of New York when they come to the aid of a woman who fears that her baby is the target of ghostly forces. Four years after Dr Peter Venkman broke up with Dana Barrett, she appeals for help to save her son when an arch-demon trapped in an oil painting wants to take over his body. The old team reunite and trace the source of the trouble to a sinister underground river of slime.

programme.title: Hook
programme.desc: Grown-up Peter Pan has to return to Neverland to rediscover his true identity in order to rescue his children from the clutches of the evil Captain Hook. Peter has grown up into a workaholic executive but takes time off for a holiday in London. Once there, his children are kidnapped by Hook, and Peter must throw off his corporate image and go back to Neverland to save his children.

programme.title: The Next Karate Kid
programme.desc: Martial arts master Miyagi has an uphill battle on his hands when he takes a troubled girl as his latest apprentice.

programme.title: Police Academy
programme.desc: Misfits realise their dreams of becoming cops when a mayoral decree forces the police academy to open its doors to all.

programme.title: Police Academy 2: Their First Assignment
programme.desc: Mahoney and friends have graduated from the Police Academy and are issued with their first assignments. As before, their cheerfulness and devotion to duty outweighs any shortcomings, but are they ready to do battle with a band of graffiti daubing terrorists?

programme.title: Police Academy 3: Back in Training
programme.desc: Slapstick farce with the incompetent cops as they set about saving their beloved academy from closure. Cutbacks mean that only one of the two police training schools can stay open, so Commandant Lassard appeals to the motley crew of graduates to come and train new recruits. However, the rival academy intends to sabotage their plans by cutting their training time down to a minimum.

programme.title: Sleepless in Seattle
programme.desc: A recently engaged woman becomes obsessed with meeting the grieving widower she heard on a late night radio show. Sam, the widower, receives hundreds of letters following the radio phone-in which was instigated by his son Jonah.

programme.title: Risky Business
programme.desc: A normally well-behaved teenager goes wild while his parents are out of town. In charge at his parents' smart house, the 17-year-old sees the chance to make a fast buck when the prostitute, on the run from a scary pimp, appears at his door. He takes her in, and, following his school's entrepreneurial teachings, lets her turn the house into a brothel.

programme.title: The Man With Two Brains
programme.desc: Zany horror spoof about a brilliant brain surgeon who falls in love with a female brain stored in a jar. The brain is part of an impressive collection held by a colleague in Austria and, upon learning of his wife's infidelity, the surgeon allows himself to be charmed. Communicating telepathically with one of the specimens in the laboratory, he falls in love - but needs a body to put it in.

programme.title: Live Die Repeat: Edge of Tomorrow
programme.desc: A soldier who was killed in action gets caught up in a time loop, in which he repeatedly relives his last battle. However, the more times he fights, the closer he gets to discovering how to defeat the alien enemy.

programme.channel: Sky Cinema Sci Fi & Horror HD.uk
programme.title: The Conjuring: Last Rites
programme.desc: In 1986 paranormal investigators Ed and Lorraine Warren travel to Pennsylvania to vanquish a demon from a family's home.

programme.channel: Sky Cinema Sci Fi & Horror HD.uk
programme.title: Pacific Rim
programme.desc: A washed-up ex-pilot and an untested trainee must drive an old, obsolete robot in a last-ditch effort to repel a lethal force of monstrous invaders.

programme.channel: Sky Cinema Sci Fi & Horror HD.uk
programme.title: Riddick
programme.desc: Left for dead on a sun-scorched planet, wanted criminal Riddick must confront two teams of mercenaries, one of whom is led by a man from his past.

programme.channel: Sky Cinema Sci Fi & Horror HD.uk
programme.title: Cloverfield
programme.desc: A leaving party for New Yorker Rob Hawkins is rudely interrupted by the arrival of an unwelcome guest, something that has a penchant for smashing up skyscrapers, playing football with the Statue of Liberty's head and handpicking humans as snacks.

programme.channel: Sky Cinema Sci Fi & Horror HD.uk
programme.title: 10 Cloverfield Lane
programme.desc: A woman discovers the horrifying truth about the outside world while living in an underground shelter with two men.

programme.channel: Sky Cinema Sci Fi & Horror HD.uk
programme.title: Nosferatu
programme.desc: A new bride is left under the care of friends when her husband travels to Transylvania for a meeting with Count Orlok. Plagued by horrific visions and an increasing sense of dread, she soon encounters an evil force that's far beyond her control.

programme.channel: Sky Cinema Sci Fi & Horror HD.uk
programme.title: Smile 2
programme.desc: Global pop sensation Skye Riley begins to experience increasingly terrifying and inexplicable events. Overwhelmed by the escalating horrors and pressures of fame, she must face her dark past to regain control of her life.

programme.channel: Sky Cinema Sci Fi & Horror HD.uk
programme.title: The Chronicles of Riddick
programme.desc: Outlaw Riddick, on the request of the peacemakers, lands on planet New Mecca and comes face to face with the army of Necromongers who are threatening all planets in their path. Their leader, Lord Marshal, wishes to convert all humans to his beliefs, or kill them.

programme.channel: Sky Cinema Sci Fi & Horror HD.uk
programme.title: Arcadian
programme.desc: In the near future on a decimated Earth, Paul and his twin sons face terror at night when ferocious creatures awaken. When Paul is nearly killed, the boys come up with a plan for survival, using everything their father taught them to keep him alive.

programme.channel: Sky Cinema Sci Fi & Horror HD.uk
programme.title: Arcadian
programme.desc: In the near future on a decimated Earth, Paul and his twin sons face terror at night when ferocious creatures awaken. When Paul is nearly killed, the boys come up with a plan for survival, using everything their father taught them to keep him alive.

programme.channel: Sky Cinema Sci Fi & Horror HD.uk
programme.title: Gator Creek

programme.title: Safe House
programme.desc: Six government agents become trapped inside a high-security safe house after a terrorist attack in downtown Los Angeles. They soon realise they must survive not just the enemy outside, but the potential traitor among them.

programme.title: Lethal Weapon 4
programme.desc: Detective Riggs and Murtaugh are called into action to foil a Chinese crime lord who is selling immigrants into slavery on American soil. The pair aren't as young as they used to be, and ending up on a Triad hit list doesn't make things easy for them either.

programme.title: Juror #2
programme.desc: A juror for a high-profile murder trial finds himself struggling with a serious moral dilemma that could influence the verdict and potentially convict, or free, the accused killer.

programme.title: Jaws 2
programme.desc: Another great white shark arrives to terrorise the holiday resort of Amity Island. Having recovered from the grisly events of four years ago, the island is once again packed with tourists. Families are sailing in the bay and swimming off the beach, but when two divers and a water-skier go missing, the local police chief fears that he is about to relive his greatest nightmare.

programme.title: Cellular
programme.desc: When teacher Jessica is kidnapped, the only call she can make on her mobile is to total stranger Ryan. It doesn't take long for Ryan to realise that only he can save Jessica - and her family - from the bad guys. She can't tell him her location, and his mobile battery won't last forever.

programme.title: The Bourne Identity
programme.desc: An amnesiac is rescued at sea by the crew of an Italian fishing boat. Nearly dead, he carries nothing but the bullets in his back and the bank account number embedded in his hip. Although completely without identity or background, he possesses an array of extraordinary talents in fighting, linguistics and self-defence that speak of a dangerous past.

programme.title: The Bourne Supremacy
programme.desc: Life never becomes easy for ex-CIA agent Jason Bourne, who is forced out of hiding, and must return to Europe to find out exactly what happened in his past. It's not easy, as he's being framed for murder and his old employers are leading the manhunt.

programme.title: The Bourne Ultimatum
programme.desc: Former CIA assassin Jason Bourne continues his quest to fill in memory gaps. When a `Guardian' reporter stumbles onto his trail, Bourne spies his chance to blow the Blackbriar black-ops brigade wide open. With some very powerful people intent on keeping their skeletons buried, he is in more danger than ever before, as his former paymasters send a series of elite killers after their reformed ex-colleague.

programme.title: The Bourne Legacy
programme.desc: Enhanced CIA assassin Aaron Cross faces a battle to survive when his CIA employers decide to do away with his programme.

programme.title: Jason Bourne
programme.desc: Living off the grid for 10 years, former CIA operative Jason Bourne finds himself back in action battling a sinister network that utilises terror and technology to maintain unchecked power.

programme.title: Safe House
programme.desc: Six government agents become trapped inside a high-security safe house after a terrorist attack in downtown Los Angeles. They soon realise they must survive not just the enemy outside, but the potential traitor among them.

programme.title: The Krays
programme.desc: The disturbing story of the making of the infamous 60s underworld figures Reggie and Ronnie Kray - from their upbringing by a domineering mother in the deprivation of London's East End. The gangland empire they build is characterised by violence and intimidation, and the film is unflinching in its depiction of their rise to power.

When the house of leaves comes crashing down, Nelson wastes no time in telling Nigel off.

programme.title: 64 Zoo Lane
programme.sub-title: The Story of Gertie the Goat
programme.desc: Gertie the goat has agreed to take Bao Bao the panda on a climbing expedition up the So Hi Mountains. The other animals advise Gertie against the trip, since pandas are not renowned for their climbing skills. Will Gertie and Bao Bao prove the others wrong, or will they need some help from their friends?

programme.title: 64 Zoo Lane
programme.sub-title: The Story of the Jungle Ball
programme.desc: The Jungle Ball is imminent and all the animals are busy finding their dance partners for the night. All except Reginald - nobody has ever asked him to dance with them at the Jungle Ball. He sets off in search of a dance partner, but has he left it too late?

programme.title: 64 Zoo Lane
programme.sub-title: The Story of the Great Rains
programme.desc: The savannah animals are busy preparing for the great rains, which are due any day. The rains flood the plains every year and they will have to take shelter on Blue Mountain. This year it is Giggles and Tickles' turn to keep watch for the start of the rain.

programme.title: 64 Zoo Lane
programme.desc: Nelson is in an inventive mood, and agrees to make various objects for his friends. Alan the aardvark is excited about the scooter that Nelson is making him - even though he doesn't really know what a scooter is. The other animals all take it in turns to help deliver the scooter to Alan, each making their own modifications to the vehicle on the way. Alan is delighted when his very own scooter eventually arrives.

programme.title: 64 Zoo Lane
programme.sub-title: The Story of Reginald's New Look
programme.desc: Nathalie the antelope decides to give Reginald the lion a makeover to refresh his look. She restyles him to such an extent that none of the other animals recognise him when he tries to alert them of an imminent danger. Will Reginald be able to lead his friends to safety in time?

programme.title: 64 Zoo Lane

programme.desc: Endure winter and early spring on the west coast, where animals eke out a chilly existence. Watch red deer battle on windswept Inishvickillane, welcome seal pups amid howling storms on the Inishkea islands, and view the return of the basking sharks.

programme.title: Pride Rules
programme.desc: Contend with the challenges of the rainy season, which turns everything green and spoils the lions' camouflage.

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: Diabetic golden-cheeked gibbon Alex needs dental treatment but the operation is complicated by her condition and chimpanzee Eddi sends a wild intruder packing.

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: Newly arrived lemur Ben meets boss lady Fennel and conducts himself accordingly while baby orangutans Kiwi and Sibu explore a tunnel full of browse.

programme.desc: Jess and Dan catch a keelback snake before it spreads its foul smell. It's a battle of wills as Mick and Adele tackle a python in a drain. And Stu discovers a dangerous snake hiding in the wall of a university.

programme.desc: The team investigate the Edinburgh Vaults, an underground tunnel system in the South Bridge that used to house hundreds of people in the 1800s, and where serial killers Burke and Hare reportedly sought out victims.

programme.title: Most Haunted
programme.desc: The team hold a vigil at Leith Hall, a manor house in Scotland that is reportedly haunted by the ghost of Laird John Leith III who was killed on Christmas Day in 1763 during a drunken brawl in which he was shot in the head.

programme.title: Most Haunted
programme.desc: The team visit Mary Kings Close, a 17th-century building built over the top of older streets and houses. Many of its residents died of the plague and a fire raged in 1750 - a child's head, a severed arm and a little girl in a blue dress are just some of the apparitions reported.

programme.title: Most Haunted
programme.desc: Yvette Fielding, Derek Acorah and a team of paranormal experts spend 24 hours in the Wellington Hotel, which was originally a 17th century coaching inn and is supposedly haunted by Thomas Hardy as well as many other ghosts.

programme.title: Most Haunted
programme.desc: Yvette Fielding, Derek Acorah and the team visit the Guild Hall in Leicester, which has had many uses over the years - including a police headquarters, a town hall and a school - and as a result is haunted by a wide range of spirits.

programme.title: Most Haunted
programme.desc: The team visit the Greengate Brewery, whose reported paranormal happenings include a woman sitting at the boardroom table, a security guard who had his face slapped when he was alone in the building, barrels moving on their own in the cellar and a dark menacing figure in the attic.
