programme.desc: After a brief conversation between Clay and a client who is about to be hung, the marshal mistakenly believes that Clay was told where the money from the heist is hidden.

programme.title: I Love Trouble
programme.desc: A hard-boiled private investigator finds lots of trouble while on a missing persons case.

programme.title: Who Killed the Cat?
programme.desc: A scheming widow tries to persecute three old ladies and poisons the cat of one of them, but fate takes its revenge on her.

programme.title: The Monkey's Paw
programme.desc: A curio dealer sells a monkey's paw that can grant the possessor three wishes but warns that disaster will follow.

programme.title: Tread Carefully
programme.desc: 50s road tyre safety. Discussed with professor Unwin and Raymond Baxter of 'Tomorrows World'.

programme.title: Four Star Playhouse
programme.desc: Businessman Carl is a good boss for 364 days a year, but his mood changes when Christmas arrives.

programme.title: Sergeant Cork
programme.sub-title: The Case of the Big Ben Lewis
programme.desc: Six men are to be hanged for murdering Sir James Gilbert. Big Ben Lewis is fighting their case.

programme.title: The Abominable Snowman
programme.desc: An adventurer and a botanist head for the high Himalayas in search of the Yeti, a legendary apelike creature believed to roam the mountains.

programme.title: The Ship That Died of Shame
programme.desc: A trio of ex-servicemen begin smuggling innocuous black market items into post-war Britain, but through greed they graduate to more sinister cargo.

programme.title: Railway Murders
programme.desc: Investigating the most notorious murders ever to take place on the British railways. Nicholas Day narrates the history of blood on the tracks of Britain.

programme.title: Strange But True
programme.desc: Michael Aspel delves into another fascinating and extraordinary story - how Etta Smith succeeded where the Los Angeles Police Department failed with her psychic powers.

programme.title: The Brothers
programme.desc: The Hammond Board vote to buy out Harry Carter. Ann plays her final hand in her game to get Brian to take her back.

programme.title: Saber of London
programme.desc: After a diamond robbery Mark Saber is called in to investigate. The only suspect is an ageing employee, but he is blind...

programme.title: The Wheeltappers and Shunters Social Club
programme.desc: Freddie Garrity sings Try a Little Tenderness and Good Morning Starshine. Barbara Law performs Happy Heart, and Tessie O'Shea sings three numbers.

programme.title: Angel on My Shoulder
programme.desc: A murdered gangster makes a deal with the devil, agreeing to take over and corrupt the identity of a crusading judge, in return for having his murder avenged.

programme.title: Nor the Moon by Night
programme.desc: A woman pursues the safari guide she thought she loved.

programme.title: Doomwatch
programme.desc: The Iron Doctor. 1971. Stars John Nolan, Jean Trend & Keith Grenville. A local hospital introduces a new electronic monitoring system for its patients - but is the machine failing?

programme.title: Burke's Law
programme.desc: A peculiar knife and a two-faced key provide clues to the murder of a famous industrial designer.

programme.title: Danger UXB
programme.desc: Doctor Gillespie is a boffin working on a new device to combat a lethal new German booby trap. Brian Ash and his section become the reluctant guinea pigs of this experiment.

programme.title: Three Silent Men
programme.desc: An inventor of a deadly weapon to be used against the allies is injured in a crash. Surgeon, Sir James saves his life but learns of his plot.

programme.title: Cinebox Memories
programme.desc: Dave Storm and The Diamonds perform the sensational hit 'If You Gotta Make a Fool of Somebody'.

programme.title: A Case for PC 49
programme.desc: A model's scheme to murder a millionaire, with the intention of inheriting his fortune, is uncovered by a police constable.

programme.title: The Royal County 1958
programme.desc: The whole of Norfolk is unique; its series of interconnected waterways called the 'Broads' are just part of its tremendous attraction which is captured here.

programme.title: Behemoth the Sea Monster
programme.desc: Atomic marine tests cause the resurrection of a dormant sea monster who then threatens to destroy London.

programme.title: The Lucy Show
programme.desc: When poor trucker Chuck Willis is turned down for a loan, Lucy decides to help him by entering him in a Robert Goulet look-alike contest. He loses the contest, but then again, so does the real Robert Goulet.

programme.title: Four Star Playhouse
programme.desc: A test pilot learns that his sick daughter could have polio, just before testing a new military plane.

programme.sub-title: The Case of the Amateur Spy

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Olivia and Alex have just got engaged and bought their first home. As a generous wedding gift, their parents have decided to fund the garden renovation. As they are providing the dosh, it will be up to them to choose the winning design. Olivia describes their current garden as a 'tidy mess'. The uneven back portion is an eyesore, and they dislike the straight, purposeless path.

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Chris and Charlie go head-to-head, redesigning a garden for a family who love art and upcycling. Claire and James's garden contains an art studio at the back, which Chris's design incorporates, while Charlie turns this area of the garden into an art-inspired gravel garden. Meanwhile, Chris's pièce de résistance is a crazy-paving patio and path made from old broken paving slabs. With a mini pond and elegant love seat thrown in, it's not long before the family have the garden of their dreams.

programme.title: My Floating Home
programme.desc: A German couple build a novel high-tech towable caravan that floats on water, in a bid to bring them closer to nature.

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Alan adds some magic to an outdoor space by showing how to get the look of a fantasy garden. He demonstrates how to build enchanting winding pathways, how to place sculpture and how to dress elegant archways. Plus, Charlote Uhlenbroek reveals how to fill the garden with birdsong and Matt James visits a reclamation yard in search of something special for his garden.

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: In this edition, Alan reveals how to identify annuals and perennials, and the best way to plant flowers to achieve a blaze of colour and scent. He also demonstrates how a sail can be used to create an amazing seating area. Plus Valentine Warner makes chocolates with lavender and Charlotte Uhlenbroek visits a beautiful butterfly garden.

programme.title: Great British Food
programme.desc: Tom celebrates the influence of Indian food on British cuisine, as he goes shopping with a spice and chutney queen and whips up vegetable pakoras.

programme.title: Great British Food
programme.desc: Tom meets an expert mushroom forager, joining her on an autumn foray before whipping up wild mushroom soup and sausage and mash.

programme.title: Country House Auction
programme.desc: Castle Durrow sets the stage, as four unique antiques have their moment under the hammer. A Fender guitar signed by the Rolling Stones piques the interest of dealer Ian. And a 400 year-old Tibetan dagger made from a meteorite prompts a bidding war.

programme.title: Celebrity Coach Trip
programme.desc: Two newbies make a big impression, while a port-tasting session loosens lips.

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Alan shows you how to create a garden thats perfect for a party. He reveals how to build an elegant bridge for your guests, how to plant around your seating areas and how to use dramatic garden lighting. Meanwhile Matt James and Valentine Warner join forces to create an alternative to the traditional barbecue, building a firepit and cooking a stuffed leg of lamb.

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Alan comes to the aid of wounded war hero Luke Simpson who lost his leg to an IED on his last tour of duty in Afghanistan, plus Frances Tophill reveals the key design elements for a cottage style garden, Katie Rushworth tells us how to make the most out of cut flowers and David Domoney gives hints and tips on how to maintain the perfect lawn.

programme.title: Garden Rescue

It's so big, it needs to be anchored to the wall. While Chris manages that and the giant bespoke bench going underneath, Flo is on rabbit duty, creating a play area for the other garden owners.

programme.desc: Rick Stein ends his series of Long Weekends in Palermo on the northern coast of Sicily with unforgettable dishes flavoured with pine nuts, raisins, lemons and almonds.

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy tests the Chrysler Crossfire and Honda's Type R Civic and NSX, Richard puts a new Smart Car - complete with two engines and two turbos - through its paces, and James casts his eye over the Jaguar XJ6. Carol Vorderman does the honours as the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car.

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: The race to Monte Carlo is on, with Jeremy in the new Aston Martin DB9, and Richard and James travelling by high speed train. James goes undercover to drive a Rover CityRover and the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car returns with actress Fay Ripley behind the wheel.

programme.title: Mortimer and Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Paul fishes at Christchurch's quintessential Bridge Pool in Dorset, hoping to catch sea trout. Bob arrives late but redeems himself by hooking the first fish of the day. After a glorious morning Paul persuades Bob to charter a boat out to sea in search of sea bass. Bob has a history of seasickness and is not keen but eventually they venture out to the Needles where Paul makes an incredible catch. The fish is too big for Bob to handle so they head off to a local restaurant to prepare it.

programme.title: Mediterranean With Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Simon Reeve embarks on the second leg of his four-part journey around the Mediterranean. Setting off from a snowy mountain ski resort in Cyprus, Simon finds an island and a capital city still deeply divided between Turkish Cypriots in the north and Greek Cypriots in the south. He then joins the UN troops who patrol the line that separates the two sides.

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Dave and Si reveal that there is so much more to British fowl than roast chicken. They cook up a storm with a duck tikka and one pot partridge recipe before tasting a slice of history from a traditional Yorkshire Christmas Pie packed with delicious birds. The Bikers also recount the history behind the famed Norfolk Black Turkey.

programme.title: Rick Stein's Long Weekends
programme.desc: Rick visits Bologna in northern Italy, a city so famous for its food that it's known as the stomach of Italy. He learns how to make the local fresh egg pasta used for tortellini, tagliatelle, ravioli and lasagne. There's stuffed rabbit, filled with Parma ham and parmesan frittata for dinner.

programme.title: Rick Stein's Long Weekends

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy sets out to compare the current Mercedes SL with the same model from 1972, and does the same with the modern-day Audi TT V6 and Ford's classic Capri V6. How do they fare? programme.title: Mortimer and Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: While in Norfolk fishing for tench, Bob tries to impress Paul with his childhood fishing rod, and fails. They share nostalgia for their younger years and reveal how they recently came face to face with their own mortality. After a brief respite in a local brewery, they reluctantly camp down in Yurts before fishing again the next day where the elusive Tench seems to evade them until the very last minute.

programme.title: Mediterranean With Simon Reeve

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Dave and Si pay tribute to Britain's dairy industry in all of its forms. The Bikers prepare a classic cheese and onion turnover and a delicious trinity cream, before making some historic British milk puddings and revisit the history of school milk and British cheddar.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: The Green Green Grass
programme.desc: While searching through some ancient scrolls and paperwork left behind by the old squire, Boycie discovers a medieval recipe for a traditional local liquor, which comes accompanied by a royal charter allowing him to produce and sell it. With the help of his staff, he goes into full-scale production, attracting customers from far and wide, including two whom he had hoped he had seen the last of.

programme.title: Ever Decreasing Circles
programme.desc: Martin is looking forward to his annual holiday in Austria, where every year he, Ann, Howard and Hilda go to the same hotel.

programme.title: Porridge
programme.desc: After reading a love letter for Bunny Warren, Fletcher imparts his wisdom to Bunny regarding the fairer sex, but it seems Fletcher doesn't know quite as much as he thinks when his daughter Ingrid brings him news from home.

programme.title: The Green Green Grass
programme.desc: Bored with life on the farm, Marlene throws herself into charity work. As a by-product of her efforts, Boycie senses an opportunity to become Sir Aubrey. Marlene, with the help of their staff, tries to use her charity contacts to help her husband achieve his dream. Unfortunately, life is very rarely that simple, particularly where Boycie and Marlene are concerned.

programme.title: Are You Being Served?
programme.desc: The staff are short of money and can't afford the new executive dining menu that is being forced on them. Mrs Slocombe has a sideline, however. A home made perfume that will attract the opposite sex!

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.sub-title: Why Does Norman Clegg Buy Ladies' Elastic Stockings?
programme.desc: Clegg has to deliver a note to Marina, but can Seymour invent something original to justify all the security?

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: A new member joins the trio of outrageous old Yorkshire boys as eccentric would-be inventor Seymour Utterthwaite gets Compo and Clegg involved with a bizarre scheme. Seymour organises the repair of a boat. What chance might there be that Compo is going to end up drenched?

programme.title: Still Open All Hours

programme.desc: The platoon arrest a supposed Nazi agent on charges of signalling to enemy aircraft.

programme.title: Still Open All Hours
programme.desc: Granville surprises Kath when she pops in for her regular loaf of bread when he claims to have a special way of matching the right product with the right customer. Meanwhile, Madge and Mavis are off to a wedding looking very glamorous and Granville gets concerned that Mavis might stay for the disco and attract new admirers, especially when Gastric recounts the story of how his cousin Maureen met her husband at a wedding.

programme.title: Still Open All Hours
programme.desc: It's a day full of bad surprises for Granville when the till becomes unwell, Leroy buys an ice cream van and the Black Widow finds him overheating in a tight spot. Meanwhile, Eric and Cyril decide to show some macho spirit and get back in the saddle.

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Lucy and Tim are distraught. Their parents' 30 year marriage is at an end and their father has moved out of the family home - and into Lee's bedroom.

programme.title: Porridge
programme.desc: The inmates of Slade Prison are horrified to discover that there is a thief in their midst. Is there no honour among thieves?

programme.title: The Green Green Grass
programme.desc: Tyler wants to borrow money to buy new equipment for his band, Puddle of Agony, but Boycie, who was brought up in the school of hard knocks, refuses to cough up. He challenges his son to stand on his own two feet and prove he is a businessman by raising the money himself. This proves difficult, with the local vicinity being gripped by fear of Animal Freedom protestors.

programme.title: Are You Being Served?
programme.desc: It looks as if Mr Humphries' career is over when money is missing from the men's counter.

programme.title: Still Open All Hours

programme.title: Still Open All Hours
programme.desc: It's a day full of bad surprises for Granville when the till becomes unwell, Leroy buys an ice cream van and the Black Widow finds him overheating in a tight spot. Meanwhile, Eric and Cyril decide to show some macho spirit and get back in the saddle.

programme.title: Still Open All Hours
programme.desc: Granville has bought a large display item to inject a little bit of sunshine into shop life on the day of Kevin's wedding. However, Kevin is the world's most reluctant bridegroom and is still having second thoughts.

programme.title: Still Open All Hours
programme.desc: It's Christmas at Arkwright's and Granville has his eye on a tree which gives him more than he bargained for. Nurse Gladys gossips with Mrs Featherstone about tactics for her new job and Eric and Cyril embark on a new fitness plan.

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Lee's former flatmate Kate has moved back to America, so Tim decides to sell the flat. Faced with homelessness, Lee announces he'll buy the flat himself and sets to work putting off any other prospective buyers, including Tim's sister Lucy. Things get worse when Lucy puts in an offer on the flat and Tim is forced to decide where his loyalties lie.

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: Agnes Brown thinks everyone has gone mad; Father Quinn is losing his faith as fast as he loses parishioners, newlyweds Dermot and Maria won't stop fighting, and Grandad has decided he'd like to witness his own funeral. The Browns decide to make Grandad happy by secretly staging his funeral, but their plans start to go awry when Mrs Brown mistakes two Mormons for the men who are supposed to be delivering Grandad's coffin.

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: Exploring traces of a mass deception hidden at Ponte Dirillo in Italy, as well as a once lavish theatre in Ohio and an ancient city with a poisonous secret.

programme.title: Smoke & Steel
programme.desc: Examining the Industrial revolution, the age of smoke and steel, a time when history's great engineers pioneered solutions that would forever change the way people live, think, and build.

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: Exploring Air Studios on the Island of Montserrat, that became a haven for rock legends.

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: Exploring Haludovo Palace Hotel in Croatia, Wolf's Lair, a German military complex and Herouni Mirror Radio Telescope - a pioneering facility in Armenia.

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: Exploring a theme park in Buenos Aires built in a time of terror and dogged by controversy, and a jaw-dropping collection of castles in Turkey.

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: Exploring Dover Commando Fortress in South East England, where traces of a top-secret Commando raid are still visible.

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: Exploring Old Byron Hot Springs in the Californian desert - a once glittering Hollywood hotspot, converted into a top-secret interrogation centre.

programme.title: Smoke & Steel
programme.desc: Examining a cliff in England that offered the public a new ride experience. Plus, an astronomical observatory in California and a mining hoist in Germany.

programme.title: Smoke & Steel
programme.desc: Examining 'The Warrior', an ironclad warship on the south coast of England. Plus, a grand public works facility in Prague and the mint building in San Francisco.

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: Exploring a dense forest near the Czech capital of Prague that hides Houska Castle, an eerie castle rumoured to possess supernatural powers.

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: Research into why and how large projects were built, the financial and social costs of their failure, and the consequences for the environment. Experts present plans to make something new from the ruins.

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: Explores a ground structure in Hull that was reduced to rubble by the planes of Hitler's Luftwaffe, Casa Albero in Italy, the USS Hornet in California, and the tunnels of Sofia in Bulgaria.

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: Explores an old landmark in Liberia with surprising American origins, the desolate town of Doel in Belgium, a Bolivian ski resort, and a pioneering scientific structure in Cleveland.

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: Explores the Dew Drop Inn in New Orleans, a music mecca that paid the price for taking on the authorities. Also explores Belgica Fort, a stronghold constructed to guard the most unlikely of treasures.

programme.desc: Dave picks up a 1970s Alfa Romeo that once starred in a chocolate advert. Meanwhile, Derek casts his eye over a Citroen 2CV before becoming distracted by a treasure trove of more than 270 motorbikes in the same garage.

programme.title: DIY SOS: The Big Build
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team are joined by designer Charlie Luxton. They enlist the help of the local community in Stoke and renovate a home for a family battling terminal illness. Dad Haydn Spice was diagnosed with abdominal cancer shortly after buying a derelict house at auction in 2004. Haydn's ill health meant that he couldn't work on the house, and his family have been left living in a building site.

programme.title: DIY SOS: The Big Build
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team enlist local tradespeople, members of the community and guest designer Charlie Luxton in Cirencester to transform an incomplete extension and run-down house into a clean, warm and healthy home for a family on the brink of despair. The Beasley family re-mortgaged their ex-council house in 2006 to pay for an extension.

programme.title: DIY SOS: The Big Build

programme.desc: Pete meets a litter of puppies known as the Potato Pups, who were found abandoned in a crisp box, and helps the staff fight to keep them alive.

programme.title: Pete Wicks: For Dogs' Sake
programme.desc: Pete meets the four-legged friends that call the UK's leading dog welfare charity, Dogs Trust, home. From when they are rescued, Pete plays an instrumental role in their rehabilitation process, right up until the point the dogs are safely rehomed.

programme.title: Pete Wicks: For Dogs' Sake
programme.desc: Pete travels to Northern Ireland to pick up three stray dogs and bring them back with him to Essex. He also meets a Shar Pei who is in constant pain and may need surgery.

programme.desc: Thomas goes down memory lane with Alison, however the truth is muddied when the other ghosts tell their version of events. Mike struggles with his own memories of Alison's past.

programme.title: Ghosts

programme.title: Ghosts

programme.title: Absolutely Fabulous
programme.desc: Since Patsy burnt down Edina's kitchen, progress on redesigning the interior has been slow. Unwisely accompanied by Patsy, Edina sets out to find the perfect door handle.

programme.title: Absolutely Fabulous
programme.desc: It's New Year's Eve in the Monsoon mansion. Edina and Patsy are planning to go to the most fabulous club on the planet, when news comes of the imminent arrival of Patsy's long-lost sister, Jackie, a woman with a reputation even worse than Patsy's own.

programme.title: Absolutely Fabulous
programme.desc: Jennifer Saunders still finds plenty of mileage out of sending up the world of fashion and PR. Edina has palpitations, when she decides that her bedroom isn't big enough. Saffy swots away on a lecture on genetics.

programme.title: Shipwreck Secrets
programme.desc: The discovery of a 19th century schooner in Lake Erie off the coast of Ohio may be the oldest wreck ever discovered. If it turns out to be the fabled Lake Serpent schooner, it could help to shed light on not just the founding of Ohio, but the settlement of North America itself.

programme.title: Shipwreck Secrets
programme.desc: Beneath the waves of the North Sea lie twenty-five shipwrecks. Relics of the biggest naval battle in history - the Battle of Jutland. It took place at the heart of the First World War and an overconfident Royal Navy expected it to be a walk-over.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Two iconic British classics find their way to the Mathewsons' auction room; Dave heads to North Manchester to pick up an Austin A30 and a Morris 8 E that's had an alternative paint job.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: New. An impressive collection of five iconic cars arrives at Thornton le Dale, including a 1967 Crayford Corsair and a 1966 Ford Zodiac which emerge from a garage in Selby.

programme.title: D-Day to Berlin
programme.desc: With British and American armies poised to cross the Rhine and Soviet forces advancing towards Berlin, victory seemed certain, and the Allies planned a new Europe based on liberty and democracy. This edition draws on the memories of those who fought, and on reconstructions to tell the story of the last desperate battles inside Germany.

programme.title: The Last Heroes of D-Day
programme.desc: In episode one of a major two-part series for BBC1, historian Dan Snow examines how two years of meticulous planning, espionage and the analysis of millions of three-dimensional aerial photographs helped the Allied Forces gain a foothold in Northern France.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team are in the Cotswolds to visit Stanway House. The team of experts make some intriguing finds, including a moving collection of love letters that tell a tragic tale from World War One, and the owner of a suite of furniture is in for a big surprise. Plus, there's a big reaction when the owner of a box of posters bought for 50p is given a current day valuation.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team of experts return to Stanway House in Gloucestershire, and are in for a busy day of valuations. Objects brought to the cameras include a painting found dumped by a motorway, a collection of Dame Barbara Cartland's hats bought by a milliner, and a very valuable self-portrait.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek inspects a collection of old police cars that includes a Rover P6 complete with radio, sirens and V8 engine. Meanwhile, Paul tries to figure out who will buy an old 1975 Range Rover that has been converted into a fire engine.

programme.desc: Derek inspects a collection of old police cars that includes a Rover P6 complete with radio, sirens and V8 engine. Meanwhile, Paul tries to figure out who will buy an old 1975 Range Rover that has been converted into a fire engine.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek swaps the chaos of the office for rain-soaked Ripon to collect a 1965 Ford Anglia with just 33,000 miles on the clock. Meanwhile, Dave enjoys the clean lines of a rare Renault Caravalle.

programme.title: D-Day to Berlin
programme.desc: With British and American armies poised to cross the Rhine and Soviet forces advancing towards Berlin, victory seemed certain, and the Allies planned a new Europe based on liberty and democracy. This edition draws on the memories of those who fought, and on reconstructions to tell the story of the last desperate battles inside Germany.

programme.title: The Last Heroes of D-Day
programme.desc: In episode one of a major two-part series for BBC1, historian Dan Snow examines how two years of meticulous planning, espionage and the analysis of millions of three-dimensional aerial photographs helped the Allied Forces gain a foothold in Northern France.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team are in the Cotswolds to visit Stanway House. The team of experts make some intriguing finds, including a moving collection of love letters that tell a tragic tale from World War One, and the owner of a suite of furniture is in for a big surprise. Plus, there's a big reaction when the owner of a box of posters bought for 50p is given a current day valuation.

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Disaster strikes when the Naughty Lass collides with a low bridge on the Bumble Hole Canal Branch damaging a chimney.

programme.title: Bangers & Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: The guys buy a confusing 1984 French Matra Murena and send it to Citroën specialists. Next, they take on a Bond classic - no, not that one - a 1977 Arctic Wetbike!

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: There's a surprise waiting for Derek in Hull - a MGSA sports car that once had a tree growing through it. Plus, in North Wales, Alan Holmes is selling his son's Ford Escort RS 1600i.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek inspects a collection of old police cars that includes a Rover P6 complete with radio, sirens and V8 engine. Meanwhile, Paul tries to figure out who will buy an old 1975 Range Rover that has been converted into a fire engine.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek swaps the chaos of the office for rain-soaked Ripon to collect a 1965 Ford Anglia with just 33,000 miles on the clock. Meanwhile, Dave enjoys the clean lines of a rare Renault Caravalle.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Two iconic British classics find their way to the Mathewsons' auction room; Dave heads to North Manchester to pick up an Austin A30 and a Morris 8 E that's had an alternative paint job.

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien are close to the Malvern Hills. Henry picks two saddles and converts them into bar stools, while Simon makes a profit on a 1960s kitchen cupboard.

programme.title: Shipwreck Secrets
programme.desc: The discovery of a 19th century schooner in Lake Erie off the coast of Ohio may be the oldest wreck ever discovered. If it turns out to be the fabled Lake Serpent schooner, it could help to shed light on not just the founding of Ohio, but the settlement of North America itself.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: How Britain Worked
programme.desc: In this new six-part series, third generation lorry mechanic, motorcycle racer and engineering enthusiast Guy Martin celebrates the workers of the Industrial Revolution by getting stuck into six of the country's biggest restoration projects. Guy's project in this opening episode is to help overhaul a tank engine, steam locomotive 5164, which is currently used on the popular Severn Valley Railway.

programme.title: How Britain Worked
programme.desc: Guy has a dual task: to get a Yorkshire saw mill - Gayle Mill in Wensleydale - up and running again, and then use it to make a replica of one of the less-celebrated inventions of the Victorian era: the first pedal-powered bicycle. Using his engineering skills, Guy helps the restoration team repair the ingenious turbine that's needed to power the whole mill, but which is currently leaking 43 litres of water a second.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Nothing screams 1970s more than a Mk3 Ford Cortina 2000E. Derek Mathewson heads to Brigg to value a 1974 example with classic Coke bottle styling and a sweet Pinto engine.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Dave goes to Wales in order to purchase a Sierra Cosworth, a sought-after car in the bidding market. Dad Derek gets his hands on a more sedate 1926 Standard Tourer - a quirky little motor.

programme.title: The Last Heroes of D-Day
programme.desc: Historian Dan Snow examines the powerful and heroic stories of those who risked their lives on the beaches of Normandy, to save the world from Nazi Germany.

programme.title: D-Day: Last Words
programme.desc: Stories of the D-Day invasion are recounted by everyone from a British sailor who laid underwater charges to blow up German defences to Canadians, Americans, French resistance fighters and Nazi defenders.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team return to Burghley House near Stamford and Abbotsford in Melrose for previously unscreened finds. Among the objects discussed are a jewel box made for, yet never given to, a royal princess, and the oldest walking stick seen on the programme. Plus, the owner of a Chinese watercolour receives a staggering valuation.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: A sheriff's ring of office, a wooden shoe collection and a painting found under a bed show up at Chester Cathedral.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
